question: How does the distribution of GDP (millions of $) vary among countries with similar ServicesRank values?

analysis.chart_type: Scatterplot (GDP vs. ServicesRank with density coloring)
analysis.design_rationale: A scatterplot directly maps each country: ServicesRank (x-axis) vs GDP (y-axis). Many countries may cluster near certain ranks or GDP levels. By encoding point density or using a colorbar, we reveal distribution shape, concentration and anomalies. This allows both overview and detail, suitable for 230 rows.
analysis.target_insight: Are higher-ranked service economies always the largest GDPs? Are there exceptions? Where are the clusters and outliers in this relationship?

chart_encodings.other_aesthetics: country label for largest outliers; colormap gradient; log GDP axis

transformations: Convert df['ServicesRank'] and df['GDP (millions of $)'] to numeric; drop rows with missing values
Apply log scale to GDP for better spread (GDP highly right-skewed)
Calculate 2D histogram of points (ServicesRank vs GDP)
Color each scatter point based on density in its (x, y) bin
data_preparation_steps: Compute 2D histogram for density coloring; assign density to each point
suggestion.matplotlib_code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib as mpl
df = pd.read_csv("csv_c_squared\1.csv")
df['ServicesRank'] = pd.to_numeric(df['ServicesRank'], errors='coerce')
df['GDP (millions of $)'] = pd.to_numeric(df['GDP (millions of $)'], errors='coerce')
df_clean = df.dropna(subset=['ServicesRank', 'GDP (millions of $)', 'Country/Economy'])
gdp_log = np.log10(df_clean['GDP (millions of $)'].values+1)
x = df_clean['ServicesRank'].values
y = gdp_log
xy = np.vstack([x, y])
kde = mpl.mlab.bivariate_normal
xmin, xmax = np.nanmin(x), np.nanmax(x)
ymin, ymax = np.nanmin(y), np.nanmax(y)
nbins = 60
hist, xedges, yedges = np.histogram2d(x, y, bins=nbins)
x_bin_idx = np.digitize(x, xedges)-1
y_bin_idx = np.digitize(y, yedges)-1
density = np.array([hist[xi, yi] if (0<=xi<nbins and 0<=yi<nbins) else 0 for xi, yi in zip(x_bin_idx, y_bin_idx)])
cmap = mpl.colormaps['viridis']
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10,7))
sc = ax.scatter(x, y, c=density, cmap=cmap, s=40, edgecolor='k', alpha=0.7)
cb = plt.colorbar(sc, ax=ax)
cb.set_label("Country Density")
for idx in np.argsort(df_clean['GDP (millions of $)'].values)[-3:]:
    ax.text(x[idx], y[idx], df_clean.iloc[idx]['Country/Economy'], fontsize=10, color='black', ha='right', va='bottom')
ax.set_xlabel("ServicesRank (lower = stronger services sector)")
ax.set_ylabel("Log10(GDP (millions of $)+1)")
title = "Scatterplot of ServicesRank vs GDP (millions of $)" + "\n" + "Point color = country density in (rank, log GDP) space" + "\n" + "Largest GDPs labeled"
ax.set_title(title)
ax.invert_xaxis()
ax.grid(True, linestyle='--', alpha=0.3)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
